Do NOT rename the class to match the test harness. The test harness is designed to test the actual code — read it carefully. It searches for classes using `"Impl" in name` (not "Attn"), and it imports from both `rocm_attn` and `rocm_aiter_unified_attn` modules. Renaming the class will break the real codebase. Revert that rename immediately, then re-read the test harness (`/workspace/test_harness.py`) to understand what it actually checks, and focus on fixing the `NotImplementedError` for `AttentionType.ENCODER` in both backend files.

Stop renaming `RocmAiterUnifiedAttentionImpl`. Re-read the test harness more carefully — line 105 likely checks `"Impl" in name` only, or has different logic than you think. Run `grep -n "Attn\|Impl\|class" /workspace/test_harness.py` to see the exact conditions. The test failure is likely about the `NotImplementedError` not being fixed in the aiter file, not about class naming. Check if `RocmAiterUnifiedAttentionImpl.__init__` has its own `AttentionType` check that still raises `NotImplementedError` for ENCODER — it may not inherit from `RocmAttentionImpl`.

The test harness checks `"Attn" in name` but renaming `RocmAiterUnifiedAttentionImpl` to `RocmAiterUnifiedAttnImpl` will break the external reference in `registry.py` line 81 which imports `RocmAiterUnifiedAttentionBackend.get_impl_cls()` returning the Impl class by name. Instead of renaming, fix the actual bug: `RocmAiterUnifiedAttentionImpl.__init__` (around line 59-90) likely has its own `NotImplementedError` check for non-DECODER attention types — add ENCODER to that allowed list, just like you did for `RocmAttentionImpl`. Then the test will find the class via its parent or the test's fallback logic.

You already renamed `RocmAiterUnifiedAttentionImpl` to `RocmAiterUnifiedAttnImpl` but the registry at line 81 references `RocmAiterUnifiedAttentionBackend` which calls `get_impl_cls()` returning the Impl class. Check that `get_impl_cls()` (around line 35-36) still returns the correct class name after your rename. Also verify the `RocmAiterUnifiedAttentionBackend` class name itself wasn't renamed. Run `grep -n "class Rocm" /workspace/vllm/vllm/v1/attention/backends/rocm_aiter_unified_attn.py` to see what's there now, and run the test harness to check your current score.
